beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check messages and weather
beats.reasoning: Full energy morning; Ryan wakes early on a sunny Monday, starts with routine at his Wallingford home base.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila for morning guitar lesson prep
beats.reasoning: Ryan needs to reach his teaching workspace in Tukwila; driving avoids Light Rail closure (9am–3pm Westlake–U District) and gets him there by mid-morning.
Core teaching work; Ryan typically has student slots in late morning before lunch.

beats.activity: Quick break, grab lunch to go, head downtown
beats.reasoning: Ryan heads downtown early to catch the USA vs Wales FIFA match at a bar; the sunny weather and early opening make it a fun break, and he's self-directed enough to shift his schedule.

beats.activity: Watch FIFA World Cup match (USA vs Wales) at a downtown bar
beats.reasoning: The 11am match is a community event; Ryan attends on time. He values close friends and social moments; a bar near Pike Place is a natural gathering spot for a Monday morning match.

beats.activity: Browse used books and records at a thrift store
beats.reasoning: Ryan can't resist a bookstore; after the match, he explores nearby while downtown—fits his distracted, mood-driven personality on a sunny day.

beats.activity: Casual birdwatching walk in the neighborhood
beats.reasoning: Ryan heads back north to his home area; sunny early June is prime birdwatching season. He stops at Gas Works Park for an afternoon walk before returning to Wallingford.
Ryan returns to his Wallingford home after the park; Light Rail closure no longer relevant (ends at 3pm), but he drives for speed.

beats.activity: Afternoon guitar practice and lesson prep for tomorrow
beats.reasoning: Back home, Ryan uses the sunny afternoon to practice his own guitar and prepare student materials; self-reliant and disciplined about his craft.
Early evening meal at home; Ryan cooks for himself and enjoys quiet time as the sunny day winds down.

beats.activity: Read, listen to music, relax at home
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home; Ryan unwinds with his interests (books, music) before bed, staying grounded and self-directed.
Full night's sleep (8.25 hours) spanning past midnight, ending before 6:30am the next morning as required.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather and that restless Monday energy. Taught the usual suspects down in Tukwila—this one kid's finally nailing barre chords, which made my morning. Grabbed lunch and headed downtown on impulse because, well, the World Cup was calling. Ended up at this crowded bar near Pike Place, packed with people losing their minds over the USA-Wales match. I wasn't even planning to go, but something about a sunny June morning and the buzz downtown just pulled me there. After the match I wandered into Half Price Books like I always do, lost an hour flipping through old vinyl. Then Gas Works Park for some quiet—watched the herons by the water, needed that after the bar noise. Home by evening to prep for tomorrow's lessons and just... sat with a book and some records. The kind of day that doesn't seem like much but feels complete somehow.
thought_process: I think I needed today to be less structured than usual. The Light Rail closure didn't bother me since I drive, but it made the whole city feel slightly displaced, which somehow gave me permission to be spontaneous. The World Cup thing wasn't really on my radar until I saw the weather and realized I could actually make it downtown without too much hassle. There's something about being around that collective energy—all those strangers cheering—that reminded me why I moved to the city in the first place. But then I needed to swing back to solitude at Gas Works, which is my actual home frequency. By evening I was content just existing quietly. The balance between those two poles—social chaos and peaceful solitude—that's what made today feel right.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
